It is a famous anecdote. Tesla later wrote that he saw a miracle. A sheet of light. A crackle of sparks that followed his hand. His father told him it was electricity. The same force that made the lightning.

items.text: Edison's work is characterized by a struggle against the challenges posed by nature.

He did not use a drafting board. He did not build models. He did not iterate. He would sit in his chair, often for hours, in a state of rigid silence. His assistants knew not to disturb him. They described his eyes as fixed on a point that wasn't there. He called this process picture thinking. He told reporters that he could build a turbine in his mind, run it for weeks, inspect the blades for wear, check the bearings for friction, and then build it in steel and it would run exactly as it did in his head.
items.gemini_reasoning: These are widely known and documented aspects of Tesla's unique mental visualization and invention process, corroborated by his own statements and assistant observations.
